outliers.caption:
I’ve been a surgeon for over 20 years, and people assume my family rules are strict.

They’re not.

My daughter makes her own choices, but these are the 8 things I’d guide her on:

1. I won’t let her think no family history means no risk. Most women diagnosed have none.
2. I won’t let her think healthy means perfect. Risk can be lowered, not removed.
3. I won’t let her see alcohol as harmless. It

after coaching 1,000+ creators, ive noticed asian creators have these strengths/weaknesses: 

strengths: 
• hard working, dilligent
• high attention to detail, better for editing/cinematics
 
weaknesses: 
• monotonous tonality
• shy, more “uptight” feeling to the way they talk on camera

i grew up poor & my parents divorced because of money problems

so i saw how important money was to keeping a family together 

and therefore i became my family’s first millionaire from zero

no connections, no handouts, just a lot of relentless work with the mission to serve my loved ones

how to escape a slump

if you skip, you’ll never win.

comment if you would join my 21-day storytelling content challenge! 

would consist of 9 frameworks for talking videos, non-talking videos, and carousels AND daily templates to help you put into action

it would also come with 10 other lessons on the principles of storytelling such as arcs, editing, psychology, filming, and so much more!

when i met @justin.leung17 2 years ago,
 
i was a recent college dropout, and he was a sophomore in college studying premed. 

we met at a intern event hosted by my friend daniel min, and justin told me he’d love to give me any free help on my business, since he had a little experience with sales

reluctantly, i said sure, and showed up like 2 minutes late the next day to our zoom coffee chat
 
bu

how to go viral as a 5 year-old 
 
1. Research viral videos - find an outlier in your “format” (100K+ views)

2. Switch their hook to use key words from YOUR niche
(example: “trading” “viral reels”)

3. Minimum Viable Editing in CapCut
 
4. Just hit post bruh

I’ve been a surgeon for 20+ years. These are the 5 symptoms patients bring in most, terrified it’s cancer. 

Here’s what the evidence says.

1. Sore, achy, heavy boobs (especially before your period) feel alarming but pain is one of the least likely symptoms to mean cancer. Cancer, when it does show up, usually doesn’t hurt at all.

You want to raise from VCs?

why do i love my girlfriend so much?

storytelling is all around us

it’s how we become more memorable, confident, and attractive

❌  it’s not about you wanting to be younger, more charismatic, or more natural on camera

❌ it’s certainly not about you needing to live a more interesting life

You have to stay in the game long enough to win.

1. Patients who ask questions and do their research are not difficult. They’re engaged and engagement is part of good care.

2. Not every breast cancer diagnosis means chemotherapy or a mastectomy. That assumption is keeping people away from screening.

3. Telling patients to stay positive is not support. Listening is.

4. A second opinion is not a betrayal of your GP. Any healthcare professional

Years of practice have shown me catching breast cancer early is rarely luck but a handful of habits, repeated consistently.

1. They know their normal. Not through obsessive checking but through familiarity. They’ve paid attention to how their breasts normally look and feel, so a genuine change stands out instead of blending in.

2. They don’t wait for a “good reason” to get something checked. The

everyone asks how i forgave my mom for leaving

the honest answer is i couldn’t

not until i flew back to korea and saw her life for what it really was

she was sick

she couldn’t speak english

she’d lost every sense of community she had here

she wasn’t leaving me behind, she was barely surviving, and korea was the only place she could breathe again

forgiveness was never about forgetting what h

faith has made me a better entrepreneur & creator

it has allowed me to detach from the outcomes

and focus only on what i can control

which in turn has also made our business more successful

but i’m humble enough to know God can take all of this away at any time

and whether he will bless us with more successes or he removes it all

for all the parents making so many sacrifices…

thank you

i would not be here without my dad working 18 hour shifts in construction

or him showing up to my soccer games in his dirty clothes

or him putting food on the table

i have always known i wanted to make it out of our struggles for him

and i’ve had the greatest privilege supporting him today

i genuinely feel like it’s my duty as a son t
